Container.Name: dozzle-agent

Branch.TagDescription: Latest upstream Dozzle with healthcheck (rebuilt weekly)

Container.Overview:
Dozzle Agent. Install this on a remote host so your main Dozzle UI can show its containers too. One main Dozzle, as many agents as you have hosts.
Set Dozzle Hostname to whatever name you want this host to show up as in the main UI. On first start the agent generates a security key in /data and prints a fingerprint to its log. Copy that fingerprint into your main Dozzle so it knows this agent is trusted.
If this is the only host, you do not need an agent. Just use the regular Dozzle template.

Config.Name: Agent Port
Config.Description: Port the agent listens on. Your main Dozzle connects to this port. Change the host side if 7007 is already taken.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: The agent reads container logs from here and sends them to the main Dozzle.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Where the agent stores its security key. Keep this around so the agent has the same identity after a restart and your main Dozzle does not have to re-add it.

Config.Name: Dozzle Hostname
Config.Description: Name this host shows up as in the main Dozzle UI. Anything that helps you tell hosts apart, like unraid-node-2.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Log level for the agent itself: debug, info, warn, error.

Config.Name: Disable Analytics
Config.Description: Disable anonymous usage analytics sent by Dozzle.
